L1.area: Data ingestion and remote resource download handling

L2.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where a filename extracted from an HTTP response header of a remote server is used to construct a local file path without proper sanitization. An attacker who controls the remote server can supply a crafted filename containing directory traversal sequences or absolute path components, resulting in arbitrary file write on the client machine and potential remote code execution.

L3.area: HTTP-based dataset source download and local file path construction
L3.description: A path traversal vulnerability exists where a filename extracted from the Content-Disposition header of an HTTP response is used to construct a local file path without proper sanitization. When loading a dataset from an HTTP/HTTPS URL, an attacker who controls the remote server can supply a crafted filename containing directory traversal sequences or absolute path components, resulting in arbitrary file write on the client machine and potential remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The filename from the Content-Disposition HTTP header is used to build a local file path without sanitization, allowing an attacker-controlled server to write arbitrary files via path traversal sequences.
